indication: Acute worsening dyspnea and pleuritic chest discomfort, heart failure with reduced ejection fraction, atrial fibrillation. CT angiography deferred due to CKD Stage 3b (eGFR 36). Evaluate for pulmonary embolism.
technique: Ventilation imaging was performed following inhalation of Tc-99m DTPA aerosol. Perfusion imaging was performed following intravenous administration of Tc-99m MAA (4 mCi) in 8 standard projections.

findings.ventilation: Homogeneous ventilation throughout both lungs. No focal ventilation defects identified. Mild central deposition of aerosol.
findings.perfusion: Homogeneous perfusion throughout both lungs without segmental or subsegmental perfusion defects. Mild heterogeneous perfusion at the bilateral lung bases, likely secondary to dependent atelectasis and small pleural effusions.
findings.matching: No ventilation-perfusion mismatches identified. Basilar perfusion heterogeneity is matched on ventilation images.
findings.chestXray: Concurrent chest radiograph demonstrates cardiomegaly with four-chamber enlargement, mild pulmonary vascular congestion, small bilateral pleural effusions (right greater than left), and bibasilar atelectasis. Findings consistent with congestive heart failure.

impression: Low probability for pulmonary embolism. No segmental or subsegmental ventilation-perfusion mismatches.
Bilateral basilar perfusion heterogeneity is matched on ventilation imaging and attributed to atelectasis and pleural effusions in the setting of heart failure.
Concurrent chest radiograph findings consistent with congestive heart failure exacerbation.
recommendations: Acute dyspnea most likely related to heart failure exacerbation
Optimize diuretic therapy and heart failure management
If clinical suspicion remains high, consider lower extremity venous duplex ultrasound
comparison: V/Q scan dated 2023-05-26. Similar low-probability findings. Basilar heterogeneity appears mildly more prominent, likely reflecting progression of heart failure.
